metadata.jumi.testShortcut: This fixture is a Sprint 3A consumer materialization smoke. The producer does not publish to the HTTP source at runtime; a pre-seeded simple_http artifact source is used instead.
metadata.jumi.obsoleteCompatibility: The consumer uses nan via the runtime shortcut image only to validate remote_fetch materialization. This is a VM happy-path profile, not the final artifact source design.

command:
mkdir -p /out; dd if=/dev/zero of=/out/report bs=1048576 count=1; mkdir -p "$(dirname "$JUMI_OUTPUT_MANIFEST_PATH")"; cat > "$JUMI_OUTPUT_MANIFEST_PATH" <<EOF
{"schemaVersion":"nan.artifactManifest.v1","runId":"${JUMI_RUN_ID}","sampleRunId":"${JUMI_SAMPLE_RUN_ID}","nodeId":"${JUMI_NODE_ID}","attemptId":"${JUMI_ATTEMPT_ID}","containerName":"main","nanVersion":"manual-vm-profile","createdAt":"2026-05-22T00:00:00Z","outputRoot":"/out","artifacts":[{"outputName":"report","declaredPath":"report","absolutePath":"/out/report","uri":"http://simple-http-artifact-source/artifacts/dataset-1mb.bin","digest":"sha256:30e14955ebf1352266dc2ff8067e68104607e750abb9d3b36582b8af909fcb58","sizeBytes":1048576,"type":"file"}]}
EOF
cat "$JUMI_OUTPUT_MANIFEST_PATH" > /dev/termination-log

mkdir -p /out; path=/work/inputs/dataset; printf 'contract=%s localPath=%s\n' "${JUMI_NODE_CONTRACT_PATH:-/jumi/node-contract.json}" "$path"; test -f "$path"; sha256sum "$path"; wc -c "$path"; cp "$path" /out/consumed
